advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm environment resolution scopes to the reviewed project's dependency set rather than the reviewer interpreter's arbitrary venv, or document/accept the reviewer-env assumption explicitly.
evidence: environment.py:92 `spec = importlib.util.find_spec(module)` resolves against the running interpreter's sys.path/site-packages
runner.py:239-245 passes `cfg.repo_path` to `grounding_tools`, but resolve_in_environment does not scope importlib to that path — the reviewed snapshot's declared deps are never consulted
pai_tools.py:180 `root` is used only to classify repo-local vs third-party, not to constrain which environment is imported from

Docstring claim environment.py:7 'consulting the SAME Python environment the code runs against' assumes reviewer venv == reviewed project's runtime, which is not guaranteed in attested/CI review of an arbitrary snapshot
advisory.finding: Environment resolution consults the reviewer process's own running interpreter/venv, not the reviewed project's declared dependencies; a symbol importable in the reviewer's environment but absent from the reviewed code's dependency set would be REFUTED (absence disproved) even though the reviewed code cannot actually import it.
scenarios: A diff references a symbol from a package that is installed in the reviewer venv but is NOT a dependency of the code under review; the absence claim is wrongly refuted.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide whether namespace-package hits (origin=None) should count as a resolution, or require a concrete origin/loader.
evidence: environment.py:97 `return spec.origin or "namespace"`
environment.py:95-96 only `spec is None` is rejected; a namespace-package spec (origin None) passes
Implicit namespace packages are located for any matching directory on sys.path, giving over-broad confirmations for bare directory names
advisory.finding: `_module_origin` treats a namespace package (spec.origin is None) as a valid resolution by returning the literal 'namespace', so any bare name matching a directory on sys.path (e.g. `tests`, `docs`, or any top-level repo dir) resolves and can produce a refute even though it is not a real importable module symbol.
scenarios: A reference named after a top-level directory (e.g. `tests`, `docs`) that is on sys.path resolves as a namespace package and is refuted as 'existing'.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add coverage/guarding for dotted member references whose receiver segment collides with an installed top-level module name.
evidence: resolve.py:479-482 member/dotted names call `_refute_via_environment(ref)` before abstaining
environment.py:65-68 a dotted name is split `head.leaf` and `head` is import-probed as a module
test_environment_resolution.py:154-158 only tests `self.foo`/`cfg.some_attr` — receivers that are not installed module names
ABSENCE: no test covers a local receiver whose name collides with an installed module (e.g. a local variable named `os` or `json`)
advisory.finding: The `member`/dotted-name path now routes through environment resolution, so a local-receiver reference `x.attr` where `x` collides with an installed top-level module name (e.g. `os.path`, `io.foo`) can be refuted; the tests only exercise receivers (self, cfg) that are not importable modules, leaving the module-name-collision case unverified.
scenarios: Reviewed code names a local variable `io` or `os` and references `io.attr`; the member absence claim could be wrongly refuted via the installed module.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the environment refute detail reflect whether the origin is repo-local vs third-party/stdlib rather than asserting third-party unconditionally.
evidence: environment.py:136-138 detail hardcodes 'a third-party/stdlib symbol the repo index cannot see'
resolve_in_environment resolves ANY importable module including the editable-installed repo package (e.g. `rebar.*`), whose origin is inside the repo tree
pai_tools.py:180-181 acknowledges the repo-local case exists ('repo-local' vs 'third-party/stdlib'), but refute_via_environment's detail does not
advisory.finding: The refute detail string always describes a hit as 'a third-party/stdlib symbol the repo index cannot see', but the environment lane also resolves repo-local modules (importable via editable install); for a repo-local origin this detail is factually inaccurate.
scenarios: A repo-local module resolvable via editable install is refuted with a detail claiming it is third-party/stdlib the repo index cannot see.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test that a declared-unsupported language (and/or monkeypatched-absent ctags binary) with an env-resolvable symbol (e.g. 'yaml') returns OUTCOME_REFUTED with backend BACKEND_ENV, pinning the env fallback on the ctags-blind paths.
evidence: resolve.py:539-545 docstring: 'The environment fallback (bug 406f) must run on EVERY ctags-blind path — an unsupported language ... a build failure ... OR zero repo defs — not only the zero-defs case; otherwise a host without universal-ctags ... would abstain on a real third-party symbol'
resolve.py:602 `env = _refute_via_environment(ref)` runs before the unsupported_lang abstain (line 608) and the build-failure abstain (line 617)

Existing tests/unit/grounding/test_resolve.py:272 test_unsupported_language_abstains uses name 'SomeProgram' (not importable) and asserts only OUTCOME_ABSTAIN; tests/unit/grounding/test_resolve.py:251 test_no_ctags_binary_abstains uses 'TicketStore' (not importable) — neither proves the env fallback fires on these paths

All new refute tests pass language='python' (supported) against an empty tmp_path, so they hit only the zero-defs fall-through — ABSENCE of a test with a declared-unsupported language (or monkeypatched-absent ctags) plus a real env-resolvable symbol (e.g. 'yaml') asserting OUTCOME_REFUTED / BACKEND_ENV
advisory.finding: No test exercises the environment fallback on the ctags-blind paths (unsupported declared language, or absent-ctags/build-failure) — only the zero-repo-defs fall-through is covered. The diff's stated core fix is that the env lane must fire on EVERY ctags-blind path, but a regression restricting it back to the zero-defs branch would pass all tests.
scenarios: A refactor moves _refute_via_environment back inside the `if len(defs)==0` branch (the pre-fix location), re-breaking third-party resolution on hosts without universal-ctags — the CI default suite
A declared-language field of 'python' vs an unsupported language changing which abstain reason wins
advisory.suggested_fix: Add `test_unsupported_language_falls_back_to_environment` (declare language='Brainfuck-9000', name='yaml') and `test_absent_ctags_falls_back_to_environment` (monkeypatch r._CTAGS_BIN to a bogus binary, name='yaml') each asserting rec['outcome']==OUTCOME_REFUTED and rec['coverage']['backend']==BACKEND_ENV.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a resolve_symbol test that resolves an in-repo package (with grounding_tools rooted at the repo) and asserts the output reports 'repo-local'.
evidence: pai_tools.py:180-181 `inside = _within_root(...) if os.path.isabs(origin) else False` / `scope = "repo-local" if inside else "third-party/stdlib (site-packages)"`
resolve_symbol docstring (pai_tools.py:160-161) advertises 'with origin + whether it is repo-local or third-party'

tests/unit/test_resolve_symbol_tool.py: test_resolve_symbol_confirms_third_party_module (line ~35) and _confirms_member_via_module_arg both assert 'third-party/stdlib' in out — ABSENCE of any test asserting scope=='repo-local' for a symbol whose origin resolves inside repo_path
advisory.finding: The 'repo-local' scope branch of resolve_symbol is untested — every tool test resolves a third-party/stdlib symbol, so the inside-root classification the tool advertises has no coverage and an inverted inside/scope check would go undetected.
scenarios: A rebar in-repo module resolvable in the environment should be reported repo-local; an inverted _within_root check would mislabel it and no test would fail
advisory.suggested_fix: Call grounding_tools(<repo root>) and resolve a first-party module (e.g. 'rebar.grounding.resolve'), asserting the returned string contains 'repo-local'.
